jokes.text: Hey, beautiful.
jokes.setup: Jazz enters and immediately hits on someone
jokes.punchline: His overly smooth pickup line delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jazz's persistent romantic pursuit character trait with economical delivery

jokes.text: I love a woman with good reflexes. Makes me crazy.
jokes.setup: Jazz commenting on woman's reflexes
jokes.punchline: Turning her defensive reaction into a compliment about her 'reflexes'
jokes.explanation: Clever reframe of rejection as attraction, well-suited to Jazz's delusional confidence

jokes.text: Your mouth is saying 'get out,' but your eyes are saying 'get busy.'
jokes.setup: Hilary tells him to get out
jokes.punchline: Jazz misreads clear rejection as mixed signals using classic pickup line structure
jokes.explanation: Classic delusional pickup artist mentality, perfectly captures Jazz's character

jokes.text: That's not going to work this time, Hil, darling. Uncle Phil went to the supermarket, so he'll be gone for at least a week.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls for daddy to rescue her from Jazz
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated timeline for Uncle Phil's grocery shopping
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Uncle Phil's grocery habits, relatable family dynamic

jokes.text: You know the drill.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls Geoffrey to remove Jazz
jokes.punchline: The routine nature of Jazz's ejections from the house
jokes.explanation: Economical setup for the running gag of Jazz being thrown out repeatedly

jokes.text: Geoffrey throws Jazz out (visual gag inferred from context)
jokes.setup: Geoffrey follows through on removing Jazz
jokes.punchline: The physical comedy of Jazz being forcibly ejected
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup that becomes a series trademark moment

jokes.text: I'm supposed to be nice to everyone who likes me? How much time do you think I have?
jokes.setup: Will asks why she can't be nice to Jazz who likes her
jokes.punchline: Hilary's narcissistic response about her popularity being a burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line - reveals her vanity while being genuinely witty, excellent character integration

jokes.text: Want to kiss my boo-boo?
jokes.setup: Will explains Jazz has deep feelings and was hurt
jokes.punchline: Jazz immediately appears completely unaffected making inappropriate advances
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character consistency - undermines Will's serious speech about Jazz's sensitivity

jokes.text: If you grab me in that place again, we're engaged.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey apologizing for throwing Jazz out
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry threat about inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry British wit paired with unexpected personal boundary, good character moment

jokes.text: I'm practicing my new scam. Girls love a guy with a sports injury.
jokes.setup: Carlton appears with crutches
jokes.punchline: His calculated attempt to appear attractive through fake injury
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton scheming behavior, reveals his calculating approach to dating

jokes.text: I can't see too many honeys being impressed by: 'Babe, I busted myself up playing peewee football.'
jokes.setup: Carlton's sports injury scam
jokes.punchline: Will's accurate assessment of Carlton's unimpressive athletic credentials
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's size and athletic limitations, solid character-based humor

jokes.text: What? / I was definitely the closest.
jokes.setup: Family guessing game about Hilary's job news ends with 'Did you get fired?'
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact assessment that firing was the most likely outcome
jokes.explanation: Good timing and realistic family dynamic, Will's practical assessment lands well

jokes.text: They say Gandhi had a very similar childhood dream.
jokes.setup: Hilary describes her childhood dream of having a glamorous job that makes people jealous
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comparison to Gandhi's supposed materialism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and unexpected reference - Gandhi as materialistic is perfectly absurd contrast to Hilary's vanity

jokes.text: She won an Oscar. / Nominated.
jokes.setup: Hilary defending Marissa's credentials
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate factual correction deflating her claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic correction, economical delivery that punctures Hilary's exaggeration

jokes.text: And she was in one of the top 10 movies of all time. / Eight years ago.
jokes.setup: Hilary continues defending Marissa
jokes.punchline: Will's timing-based deflation of the achievement
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Will deflating Hilary's claims, good rhythm established

jokes.text: She needs a new assistant because she just got back from a well-deserved year off. / Nose job.
jokes.setup: Hilary's euphemistic explanation for Marissa's absence
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt revelation of the real reason
jokes.explanation: Continues the established pattern with good contrast between euphemism and reality

jokes.text: How did you know all of that? / I have an inquiring mind.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks how Will knows celebrity gossip
jokes.punchline: Will's understated way of saying he reads tabloids
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Will's street smarts and knowledge of pop culture

jokes.text: Hilary, I loved her in that musical she did. God, what a voice. / Lip-synch.
jokes.setup: Will pretends to compliment Marissa's singing
jokes.punchline: His immediate revelation that she lip-synced
jokes.explanation: Great fake-out where Will seems supportive then delivers the deflating truth, perfect timing

jokes.text: All a part of my plan.
jokes.setup: Jazz was told he left but is still there
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence that everything is calculated
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jazz's catchphrase that turns all his failures into supposed successes

jokes.text: What do you mean you give up? This is fun. No, guess again.
jokes.setup: Hilary playing an extended guessing game on the phone
jokes.punchline: Her obliviousness to her friend's frustration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Hilary's self-centered nature in social situations

jokes.text: I don't care if you have other appointments, clown. My cat needs acupuncture.
jokes.setup: Marissa on the phone establishing her personality
jokes.punchline: The absurd priority of cat acupuncture over human appointments
jokes.explanation: Establishes Marissa's entitled celebrity behavior with an absurd specific detail

jokes.text: So what fool got the part? / Meryl Streep.
jokes.setup: Marissa angrily asking who got the role she wanted
jokes.punchline: The reveal that the obviously qualified Meryl Streep got the 'Meryl Streep type' role
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - she calls whoever got the part a 'fool' then learns it's the person the role was written for

jokes.text: Ain't that cozy?
jokes.setup: Learning Meryl Streep got the Meryl Streep-type role
jokes.punchline: Marissa's sarcastic reaction to the obvious casting choice
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the Meryl Streep reveal, shows Marissa's petty reaction

jokes.text: Shut up.
jokes.setup: Hilary tries to be supportive using Marissa's own positive buzzwords
jokes.punchline: Marissa harshly shuts down the attempt at positivity she just demanded
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Marissa's hypocrisy in demanding positivity then rejecting it when offered

jokes.text: Isn't he, like, my age? / I see. I get it.
jokes.setup: Marissa wants to date young Johnny Gill, Hilary points out the age issue
jokes.punchline: Marissa's paranoid interpretation as age discrimination
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Marissa's insecurity about aging and tendency to see discrimination everywhere

jokes.text: You're walking a fine line, Helena. A very fine line.
jokes.setup: Marissa getting Hilary's name wrong again while being threatening
jokes.punchline: The combination of wrong name with dramatic threat
jokes.explanation: Continues the name confusion running gag while showing Marissa's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: You know, while you're down there... you may even get to see Dustin Hoffman's drawers.
jokes.setup: Hilary complaining about picking up dry cleaning as unglamorous
jokes.punchline: Will's pun on 'drawers' (underwear/furniture) and celebrity proximity
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that plays on the glamour/mundane contrast of celebrity dry cleaning

jokes.text: Flowers, a Kit Kat... and a song.
jokes.setup: Will presenting Jazz's romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic inclusion of a Kit Kat bar in the romantic package
jokes.explanation: Good bathos - flowers and song are romantic, Kit Kat is hilariously mundane

jokes.text: Little Willie Smith. I like that.
jokes.setup: Will introduces himself
jokes.punchline: Marissa's inappropriate sexual interest in the teenager
jokes.explanation: Establishes the uncomfortable power dynamic and Marissa's inappropriate interest

jokes.text: Don't you remember, Jazz... you've got that brain-surgery appointment.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Jazz to leave
jokes.punchline: The absurd excuse that Jazz supposedly needs brain surgery
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor that also implies Jazz needs brain surgery, working on multiple levels

jokes.text: Don't you remember, Jazz... you've got that brain-surgery appointment.
jokes.setup: Will tries to get Jazz to leave with him but Jazz doesn't pick up the hint
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly desperate and absurd excuse for Jazz to leave
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd excuses, with brain surgery being appropriately ridiculous for Jazz

jokes.text: Because if he doesn't want to go out with me tonight, you're fired, Hester.
jokes.setup: Marissa asking Will out through Hilary
jokes.punchline: The threat combined with yet another wrong name for Hilary
jokes.explanation: Continues the name gag while escalating Marissa's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I forfeit the game.
jokes.setup: Carlton playing chess on crutches
jokes.punchline: His admission of defeat, presumably due to the inconvenience of his fake injury
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Carlton's crutches gag - his scheme backfires in mundane ways

jokes.text: So what did you do this afternoon? Massage her feet, give her cat a perm?
jokes.setup: Will asking about Hilary's first day
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity from foot massage to cat perm
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of celebrity assistant clichés, callback to the cat acupuncture

jokes.text: Hilary, I wouldn't go out with her if she and Marsha Warfield were the last two women on earth.
jokes.setup: Will rejecting the idea of dating Marissa
jokes.punchline: The specific inclusion of Marsha Warfield as equally unappealing option
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that dates the show but works as character-specific humor for Will

jokes.text: I know... but I'm the envy of all my friends.
jokes.setup: Will pointing out Marissa treats her badly
jokes.punchline: Hilary valuing others' envy over her own dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - prioritizing status over self-respect, very relatable human flaw

jokes.text: And I guess it won't kill you to go out with Jazz.
jokes.setup: Will agrees to help Hilary by dating Marissa
jokes.punchline: His immediate quid pro quo putting Hilary in an equally uncomfortable position
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Will's helpful gesture comes with a price that puts Hilary in the same boat

jokes.text: Does Jazz have a last name? / I believe it's Matazz.
jokes.setup: Question about Jazz's full name
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan delivery of 'Jazz Matazz' pun
jokes.explanation: Solid pun delivered with Geoffrey's characteristic dry wit, memorable wordplay

jokes.text: I don't see the destruction of our gene pool as something to joke about.
jokes.setup: Family joking about Hilary and Jazz together
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's dramatic overreaction treating it as genetic catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Excellent Uncle Phil moment - his pompous way of expressing concern about Jazz is perfectly in character

jokes.text: As of tonight, my new official name is Mr. Lucky.
jokes.setup: Jazz arriving for his date with Hilary
jokes.punchline: His overconfident assumption that he's finally succeeded
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Jazz's delusional confidence even when the date is clearly arranged

jokes.text: Look at that. Butter on ice. I'm living large now.
jokes.setup: Will is at the fancy restaurant with Marissa
jokes.punchline: His amazement at the simple luxury of butter on ice
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Will's background - being impressed by fancy restaurant details

jokes.text: Hey, Cher! Sophomore in high school. Beat that, honey.
jokes.setup: Marissa sees Cher at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her competitive comment about dating younger men
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Marissa's competitive nature and inappropriate pride in dating teenagers

jokes.text: That was a wonderful imitation of a creepy spider you just did, Jazz.
jokes.setup: Jazz makes some romantic gesture with his hands
jokes.punchline: Hilary's cutting insult comparing his romantic move to a spider
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that's both specific and visual - comparing Jazz's romantic gesture to creepy crawling

jokes.text: It's either 'slow dancing with Jazz' time... or 'Will getting up, going home, and leaving you without a job' time.
jokes.setup: Jazz asking Hilary to dance
jokes.punchline: Will's overly elaborate way of explaining Hilary's lack of choice
jokes.explanation: Will's verbose explanation of the obvious situation works as character humor

jokes.text: It's 'slow dancing with Jazz' time.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks what time it is
jokes.punchline: Marissa creates her own time designation to force Hilary to dance with Jazz
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation tactic showing Marissa's control over the situation

jokes.text: Man, this must be my birthday.
jokes.setup: Hilary reluctantly agrees to dance
jokes.punchline: Jazz's oblivious joy at what's clearly a forced situation
jokes.explanation: Jazz's complete misreading of the situation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Champagne would obviously be the perfect choice, Einstein.
jokes.setup: Marissa angry about not sending gift to director
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic 'Einstein' while blaming Hilary for her own unclear instructions
jokes.explanation: Shows Marissa's tendency to blame others for her own poor communication

jokes.text: You're the idiot. / Well, date's over.
jokes.setup: Marissa calls Hilary an idiot
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate matter-of-fact ending of the date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - direct, no-nonsense response with great economy and timing

jokes.text: She's smart, sweet, and the most beautiful woman in the cosmos... and she's been very nice to me tonight.
jokes.setup: Jazz defends Hilary against Marissa's insults
jokes.punchline: His genuine, over-the-top praise of Hilary including 'cosmos'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character growth moment - Jazz shows genuine depth and loyalty, 'cosmos' is perfectly over-the-top

jokes.text: Six.
jokes.setup: Hilary tells Marissa her last five movies sucked
jokes.punchline: Hilary's correction making it even worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - just when the insult seems complete, Hilary makes it worse with one word

jokes.text: Really? / Psych.
jokes.setup: Jazz saying they can have fun without Will and Marissa
jokes.punchline: His immediate 'psych' revealing he was joking
jokes.explanation: Good Jazz moment - even in his moment of defending Hilary, he can't resist the joke

jokes.text: I got to go. / Peace. / Peace, babe.
jokes.setup: Will and Jazz saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: The casual 'Peace, babe' showing their friendship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Will and Jazz's genuine friendship beneath the surface

jokes.text: I'd only say 'yes' because I felt like I owed you something. That's cool with me.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks Jazz out but admits it would only be out of obligation
jokes.punchline: Jazz's acceptance of even a pity date
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Jazz's acceptance of Hilary's honesty and low standards for affection

jokes.text: I don't think it's a good time. I'd only say 'yes' because I felt like I owed you something.
jokes.setup: Jazz asking Hilary out for Saturday
jokes.punchline: Hilary's brutally honest assessment of her motivations
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment - Hilary being honest about her feelings rather than leading Jazz on

jokes.text: That's cool with me.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she'd only date him out of obligation
jokes.punchline: Jazz's acceptance of even pity dating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz response - his standards are so low that obligation-based dating sounds good to him

jokes.text: All a part of my plan.
jokes.setup: Hilary kisses Jazz after seeming to reject him
jokes.punchline: Jazz's signature catchphrase suggesting this was all calculated
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending callback - transforms Jazz's delusional catchphrase into a triumphant moment, great payoff
